avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 25 cm of snow will fall until the evening above approximately 1400 m.
The sometimes strong wind will transport the fresh and old snow. As a consequence of snowfall and the moderate to strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form in the course of the day. These will form in particular between approximately 2100 and 2500 m.
The fresh and older wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant above the tree line.

In localities where the wind is stronger danger level 4 (high) will be reached.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes strong wind will transport the new snow and, in some cases, old snow as well.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in danger of dry avalanches as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase. Even emptied starting zones will be replenished as a consequence of snowfall and wind, in particular along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland.
Especially on very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural avalanches are to be expected. Here the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach very large size.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The conditions are precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities outside marked and open pistes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 30 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 80 cm in some localities, will fall until Monday above approximately 1600 m. The wind will be strong.
The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall will become increasingly prone to triggering.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Several slab avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a slow strengthening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and stormy weather.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular along the border with France, along the border between Valais and Italy 25 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 60 cm in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1700 m.

As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form in the course of the day. The strong wind will transport the new snow and, in some cases, old snow as well.
The sometimes large wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant, above the tree line.
On steep slopes and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain the avalanche situation is very precarious.

On wind-loaded slopes and adjacent to ridgelines medium-sized to large slab avalanches are possible, in isolated cases also in case of releases originating from steep and high-altitude southeast facing starting zones that have retained the snow thus far.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs in the high Alpine regions. Wet and gliding snow at low altitude.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a moderate wind from northwesterly directions, wind slabs will form at elevated altitudes.
Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep shady slopes in high Alpine regions, as well as on wind-loaded slopes, in the regions exposed to a lot of wind, also at transitions into gullies and bowls.

In isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

On steep slopes and at low altitude more moist avalanches are possible by midday, but they can reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the last few days hard wind slabs formed over a wide area. The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

As a consequence of a moderate wind from northwesterly directions, mostly small wind slabs will form.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunshine and high temperatures gave rise to moistening of the old snowpack at low altitude.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and wind, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. They must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Loose snow avalanches are possible in particular on sunny slopes. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground. They can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be partly cloudy.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent. The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in some places be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall until the evening above approximately 1000 m.
As a consequence of snowfall and the moderate to strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form in the course of the day. The old wind slabs will be covered with new snow and therefore difficult to recognise.
Fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.

In very isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in danger of dry avalanches as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
Even emptied starting zones will be replenished as a consequence of snowfall and wind.
Especially on very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.
In some places the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Monday above approximately 1600 m. The wind will be strong.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a slow strengthening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The off-piste conditions are dangerous. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are being formed by the strong westerly wind can be released very easily. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain above the tree line. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found also in areas close to the tree line.
In some places avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
These places are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

As a consequence of the snowfall the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses. Individual natural avalanches are to be expected.

At low and intermediate altitudes small wet loose snow slides are to be expected as a consequence of the rain, in particular on cut slopes, and on very steep grassy slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Monday. As a consequence of a strong wind from northwesterly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs will form. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places.

The fresh snow and the wind slabs will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack. Stability tests and reports filed by observers confirm the existence of a weak snowack.

tendency.highlights:
Further increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.
Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Natural avalanches are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Steep, little used shady slopes are to be avoided as far as possible. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger.

Fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are prone to triggering, in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain above the tree line. They are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall. As a consequence of a strong wind from northwesterly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs will form especially in gullies and bowls. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places.

Above the tree line: The snowpack will be prone to triggering. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights:
Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Restraint is appropriate.

Some snow will fall.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Saturday above approximately 1800 m. Some mostly small natural avalanches are not ruled out. The wind was strong at times. Over a wide area large surface-area wind slabs formed. These have formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. In particular on very steep shady slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible.
In particular on very steep slopes and in gullies and bowls medium-sized avalanches are possible.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended. Experience and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of last week is lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. Weak layers deep in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls medium-sized slab avalanches are possible. In particular transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are treacherous. Mostly small natural avalanches are possible below approximately 1800 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In some places wind slabs are lying on a hard crust. Light snowfall above approximately 1500 m: In the course of the day further wind slabs will form in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The off-piste conditions are unfavourable. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

As a consequence of new snow and a strong northwesterly wind, easily released wind slabs will form in particular above the tree line, in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Individual natural avalanches are to be expected.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall on Monday. As a consequence of a strong wind from northwesterly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs will form especially in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places.
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

tendency.highlights:
Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Restraint is appropriate.

In some regions increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind. This applies in particular in the north and in the west.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. In addition the wind slabs should be taken into account.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Saturday above approximately 1800 m. The wind was strong at times. The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released easily or naturally. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach quite a large size, in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even now by people. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line, and on little used shady slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended. Experience and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes.
At high altitude the wind slabs have increased in size additionally in the last two days. The snowpack remains generally prone to triggering.
Some snow will fall in particular in the regions of the north exposed to the foehn wind.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will persist. Above the tree line a treacherous avalanche situation will prevail.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche-prone wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Loose snow avalanches are possible in particular on sunny slopes. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground.

snowpackStructure.comment: Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be partly cloudy.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies especially on steep shady slopes adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line.
As a consequence of the occasionally strong northerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. These are in some cases prone to triggering. In some cases avalanches are medium-sized.

Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint. Only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack. The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

Low and intermediate altitudes: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released in isolated cases in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular local mostly small wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.

avalancheActivity.comment: On very steep slopes hardly any loose snow avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment: A little new snow above approximately 1400 m.
